Michael,

We got both messages. We also a Gentleman's Agreement here to not overdue it

before though.

Cheers,  Dirk (aka "listmaster")

-- 
dirk.eddelbuettel.com | @eddelbuettel | edd at debian.org

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Finance] Rblpapi testing needed
messages.body_plain:
The Rblpapi package is readying a new release with updated libraries. This
would also bring, inter alia, a version for arm64 macOS.

Now, if *you* are familiar with Rblpapi, can build it from source and have
built it from source *we would love to hear from you*.  For "reasons" neither
John nor I can test it right now but as responsible stewards of the code we
would appreciate some testing prior to next steps.

In short, we have a development branch at Rblpapi will builds against a
corresponding development branch at the blp repo providing binary blobs from
Bloomberg (repackaged for our use).  A command such as

   remotes::install_github("Rblp/Rblpapi", ref="feature/release_3.24.6.1")

will do this. Windows users need Rtools, of course. You should then see

   > library(Rblpapi)    
   Rblpapi version 0.3.14.1 using Blpapi headers 3.24.6.1 and run-time 3.24.6.1. 
   Please respect the Bloomberg licensing agreement and terms of service.
   >  

Please come to the GitHub repo for Rblpapi with any questions.

Thanks,  Dirk and John

-- 
dirk.eddelbuettel.com | @eddelbuettel | edd at debian.org

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Finance] Test listsend
messages.body_plain:
Hi Dirk

Many thanks and apologies for the double-send here. This 'test' send was
not meant to go out to the full list.

In case it's of interest, if anyone would be interested in offering a
seminar or workshop with Instats <http://instats.org/>, I'd be happy to
chat about this. We collaborate with leading researchers, universities, and
professional associations (including the American Statistical Association,
Statistical Society of Canada, VSSOR, and others) to offer research
training through livestreamed and on-demand workshops to a global audience.

One nice feature of Instats is that we are all academics, so we never seek
to own our partners' IP and we're happy to host free or fee-based content.
We also believe in academic freedom, so you can offer the content you want,
in the format you prefer, and do it on your schedule. After a livestreamed
workshop, we are able to host the content for on-demand access for
streaming across the globe, and we are happy to allow all of your
institution?s students and researchers free access to your content both in
the live and on-demand spaces. This allows you to use Instats as a free
research training platform internally, while generating revenue when
fee-based content sells externally -- if you'd like this option -- and any
profit we generate for ourselves goes to our registered charity.

If anyone is interested, I'd be happy to meet for a quick chat. Instats
also has a new tutorial and demonstration of our platform?s partner
features, which make creating new content a snap with our AI-enabled
seminar builder. Feel free to check out the on-demand streaming video here
<https://instats.org/seminar/run-your-seminars-workshops-and-confer3>.


Best wishes and many thanks

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
*instats.org* <http://instats.org>
<http://instats.org>
